validation_summary.evidence: python -m py_compile exits 0 (~0.1s); python -m unittest skills.development-harness.scripts.tests.test_parallel_execution_readiness_checklist -v passes 13/13 (0.004s); python -m unittest discover skills/development-harness/scripts/tests passes 303/304 with 1 OS-specific skip (38.3s, up from 291 -- 13 new cases for unit_051)

checkpoint.summary: unit_051 complete. skills/development-harness/references/parallel-execution.md § 6 'Readiness checklist' extended with the three PHASE_012 acceptance bullets and split into two sub-headings: 'Unit-declaration readiness' (4 items including the strengthened touches_paths check + the new no-shared-aggregator check) and 'Runtime & infrastructure readiness' (5 items including the new multi-commit-CI check). Three new bullets are bolded so readers distinguish them from the pre-unit_047 baseline. Each new bullet is actionable not just descriptive: touches_paths names the 'not_parallel_safe' exclusion + phase-contract.md cross-link; shared-aggregator defines the pattern + names 3 concrete cross-stack examples (router, index.ts, __init__.py) + proposes 2 remediations (absorb into dependents OR serialize via depends_on); CI check names the 'harness: merge <unit_id>' commit-message template + 2 concrete CI anti-patterns (per-commit hooks, rate-limit) + the 'keep parallelism off until the pipeline is fixed' remediation. New test_parallel_execution_readiness_checklist.py (200 LOC, 13 cases across 5 classes) pins each new bullet's exact wording + rationale tokens + the 7 pre-unit_051 items survive the edit (regression guard against careless rewrites). Suite 291 -> 304.
